beats.activity: Wake up, coffee on the porch, check emails and garden status
beats.reasoning: Saturday morning ritual—Jackson starts slow with coffee and porch time, checking on the garden in the overcast weather. Low energy from yesterday (45/100) means he'll move at his own pace today.

beats.activity: Water and tend the garden, harvest any ripe vegetables
beats.reasoning: Garden upkeep is a weekend priority; overcast skies mean no harsh sun stress, and he'll want to check on the lettuce and other plantings before heading out.

beats.activity: Shower, get dressed, pack a messenger bag for farmers market
beats.reasoning: Quick prep before heading out; Jackson is a homebody so he won't linger, but he's deliberate about his morning routine.
Saturday farmers market is a reliable errand; Ballard is close enough from Northgate (40 min drive accounting for weekend traffic) and fits his preference for local, familiar spots over crowds.

beats.activity: Browse farmers market, chat with vendors, pick up fresh produce and flowers
beats.reasoning: Ballard market is quieter and more intimate than U-District; Jackson prefers time with familiar people over big crowds. He'll pick up produce and spend unhurried time there.
Return commute; Jackson is easily distracted and a homebody, so he'll head back to familiar territory rather than linger or explore further.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat lunch with fresh farmers market ingredients, update expense spreadsheet
beats.reasoning: Jackson values home time and meticulous record-keeping; he'll cook lunch from his market haul and track his spending—both low-key weekend activities that suit his homebody nature.

beats.activity: Rest on the porch, read a book, enjoy the overcast Saturday
beats.reasoning: The 1pm Mariners game at T-Mobile Park doesn't appeal to Jackson—he avoids big crowds and SoDo is peak Saturday traffic. He'll skip the event and enjoy quiet downtime instead.

beats.activity: Bike maintenance and repair: tune up personal bike, organize workshop tools
beats.reasoning: Weekend home workshop time; Jackson is a bike mechanic who enjoys hands-on work in his own space. This is both vocational and therapeutic for him.

beats.activity: Short neighborhood bike ride or walk around Northgate, light exercise
beats.reasoning: Overcast weather is perfect for a gentle ride or walk; Jackson stays local and close to home rather than venturing far. Low energy from yesterday means a short, mellow outing.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner with fresh garden produce, enjoy meal on the porch
beats.reasoning: Evening ritual mirrors yesterday's pattern; Jackson cooks with his harvest and savors dinner outdoors as the light fades. Reinforces his homebody, gardening-focused weekend.

diary:
Started the morning right—coffee on the porch with the garden looking decent despite the overcast. Pulled some early tomatoes and lettuce that actually came in this year. By mid-morning I was at the Ballard market, which was packed but in a good way. Ran into Maria from the flower stand; she remembered I'd asked about dahlias last month. Got some nice greens and a bunch of those purple ones she recommended.

Lunch was simple—fresh stuff, always tastes better when you know where it came from. Spent the afternoon tuning up my own bike in the workshop; the derailleur's been acting up and it felt good to finally fix it properly. Did a slow loop around the neighborhood after, nothing ambitious. The cool weather kept everything pleasant.

Dinner on the porch as the light faded. Just me and the garden, some decent food, and the quiet satisfaction of a day well-spent.
thought_process: The overcast weather actually worked in my favor—not too hot for market browsing or working in the shop. I skipped heading downtown to catch the Mariners game; the crowds would've been brutal and honestly, I'd rather spend Saturday at my own pace. The farmers market felt like the right move, especially talking to the vendors. It keeps me connected to people and seasons in a way that matters. Tuning the bike scratched an itch I've had all week—there's something centering about hands-on work. The neighborhood ride was just enough to stay loose without wearing out. By evening I was genuinely content, which doesn't always happen on Saturdays anymore.
world_event_prompt: Overcast Saturday, high of 64F. Farmers markets in Ballard, Fremont, and the U-District in full swing. Mariners home game at T-Mobile Park at 1pm brings SoDo crowds.
